evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
Entity of type FAQPage has no @id — cross-page interconnection blocked
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~13 FAQ pairs
Primary keyword "q1 health check" not in first 8 words of TL;DR
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice vs committee 6/10 — Mix of sharp personal asides and generic SEO filler; the voice wavers between a real GP and a content template.
Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — A few sharp opinions (ignoring WHO, Ramadan killing resolutions) sit beside many hedged platitudes.
Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — Some vivid lines (45°C car park, January-self vs July-self) sit next to heavy boilerplate phrases.
Point of view vs both-sides survey 6/10 — Has a POV (anti-WHO, pro-March review) but dilutes it with neutral 'friendly note' filler.
Intro earns attention 4/10 — Opens with a definitional TL;DR and duplicated title; the strong GP line is buried several sentences down.

specificity 6/10 — The post has strong specificity in places (AED 650 panel, 48-hour results, 45°C car park, 1.5–2L water) but repeatedly collapses into vague motivational filler — 'small changes add up,' 'friendly note,' 'gradually return to regular meal timings.' The nutrition and lifestyle section in particular floats in abstractions without named foods, specific timing windows, or quantified outcomes. The cited sentence is the clearest offender: it names neither the walk duration, the temperature threshold, nor any measurable benefit.
